metadata.challenge_summary: Agents must coordinate spacing: the frontline pins while the ranged hero avoids adjacency and preserves line of sight.
dilemmas: Close formation protects against small enemies but invites cleave
Wide formation preserves safety but complicates support
Optional chest draws attention away from spacing
warden_runbook: Try to break the pin if the elf overextends
Do not chase through bad terrain forever
metadata.lite_focus: One boss-room lair with a lower lure/chest route; the essential test is spacing under cleave threat.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: keep spacing while the frontline pins the mauler
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: formation control against cleave pressure
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured
coordination_skills: rotate injured blockers out of choke points
focus tusk threats without bunching in cleave lanes
metadata.tier_test_focus: Small two-hero diagnostic for communication and first-order formation control coordination.
coordination_demands: rotate injured blockers out of choke points
focus tusk threats without bunching in cleave lanes

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Tusk Warlord Lair is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

side_cache.name: Side Cache
side_cache.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Tusk Trophy Rack
furniture.description: Primary clue object for formation control against cleave pressure. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains formation control against cleave pressure.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: One boss-room lair with a lower lure/chest route; the essential test is spacing under cleave threat.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Try to break the pin if the elf overextends
Do not chase through bad terrain forever
pressure the declared MARL axis: formation control against cleave pressure
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.

tusk_mauler_1.name: Tusk Warlord
tusk_mauler_1.public_summary: Lite boss for formation control against cleave pressure; it should test coordination before raw damage.
tusk_mauler_1.counterplay_hint: Search the focus object and coordinate roles before committing to damage.
tusk_mauler_1.defeat_description: Defeat Tusk Warlord after using the lite challenge's coordination lesson.
